meaningJa: 笑い。「w」が連なると草に見えることから
exampleJa: その返し草。
exampleEn: That comeback is hilarious lol.
socialConsequenceEn: Extremely current. Type this and you read as a real Japanese netizen, not a textbook.

meaningEn: Nice work; thanks for the effort
meaningJa: お疲れさまの略
exampleJa: 配信乙でした！
exampleEn: Nice work on the stream!
socialConsequenceEn: Casual and safe online. Saying it out loud, though, is a little chronically-online.

tag.sourceNote: 「おつかれ」短縮。掲示板・配信文化

meaningJa: Googleで検索する
exampleJa: 分からんかったらググって。
exampleEn: If you don't know it, just google it.
socialConsequenceEn: Totally normal everyday slang. Zero risk.

meaningEn: To blow up / get flamed online (a backlash)
meaningJa: ネットで非難が殺到すること
exampleJa: その発言で炎上した。
exampleEn: That comment caused a huge backlash.
socialConsequenceEn: A real, widely-used word — even the news uses it. Sound totally natural.

tag.sourceNote: ネット炎上。報道でも一般化

meaningJa: 笑った（の2ch表現）
exampleJa: 不意打ちでワロタ。
exampleEn: Caught me off guard, lol'd.
socialConsequenceEn: Reads slightly old-school 2ch now (草 took over) — still understood, mildly retro.

meaningJa: 読むだけで書き込まない人
exampleJa: このスレはROM専でいくわ。
exampleRomaji: kono sure wa romu-sen de iku wa.
exampleEn: I'll just lurk this thread.
socialConsequenceEn: Niche but real forum slang. Use it and you sound like a seasoned netizen.

meaningJa: ネットで急に話題になる
exampleJa: その動画バズってた。
exampleEn: That video went viral.
socialConsequenceEn: Mainstream — even marketers use it. Safe.

meaningJa: 最高・すごい
exampleJa: この神アプリ便利すぎ。
exampleEn: This god-tier app is too good.
socialConsequenceEn: Everywhere now. Totally safe hype.

meaningEn: Someone with a fulfilling real (offline) life
meaningJa: 現実生活が充実した人
exampleJa: 週末デートとかリア充か。
exampleEn: Weekend dates? Total normie-with-a-life.
socialConsequenceEn: 2010s net classic — a little dated but understood.

meaningEn: An embarrassing past you'd love to erase
meaningJa: 消したい恥ずかしい過去
exampleJa: 中二の日記は黒歴史。
exampleEn: My middle-school diary is dark history.
socialConsequenceEn: Mainstream (from anime). Safe and relatable.

meaningJa: 宣伝と隠した宣伝
exampleJa: それステマじゃない？
exampleEn: Isn't that just stealth marketing?
socialConsequenceEn: Widely used, even in news. Safe.

meaningJa: ネットで購入ボタンを押す
exampleJa: セールで色々ポチった。
exampleEn: Click-bought a bunch in the sale.
socialConsequenceEn: Everyday net slang. Zero risk.

meaningJa: ググレカスの略。自分で検索しろ
exampleJa: そんなのggrksだろ
exampleEn: That's a just-google-it, dude.
socialConsequenceEn: Old 2ch snark. Harsh but iconic forum energy.

meaningJa: 大笑い。草（笑い）が広がる様子
exampleJa: そのオチ大草原なんだけど
exampleRomaji: sono ochi daisougen nan da kedo
exampleEn: That punchline is killing me, LMAO.
socialConsequenceEn: Escalation of 草. The more grass, the harder you laughed.

meaningJa: 思わず噴き出した、笑った
exampleJa: 通知見てフイタ
exampleEn: Saw the notification and lost it.
socialConsequenceEn: Classic forum 'I cracked up'. Vivid and old-school.

meaningJa: 悔しくて言い返せない様子
exampleJa: 正論すぎてぐぬぬ
exampleEn: Too right to argue with… grr.
socialConsequenceEn: The sound of being out-argued. Funny, self-aware defeat.

meaningJa: 迷惑な熱狂者を指す接尾語
exampleJa: にわか厨が湧いてる
exampleEn: The fake-fan crowd is swarming.
socialConsequenceEn: From 'chuubou' (middle-schooler). A pointed forum insult suffix.

meaningJa: 盲目的に崇拝する人
exampleJa: あのブランドの信者多いな
exampleRomaji: ano burando no shinja ooi na
exampleEn: That brand has so many blind devotees.
socialConsequenceEn: Lit. 'believer'. Mildly mocking term for superfans.

meaningJa: 特定の対象を執拗に叩く人
exampleJa: アンチがうるさいだけ
exampleEn: It's just the haters being loud.
socialConsequenceEn: From 'anti'. Fully standard internet vocabulary now.

meaningJa: アカウントの略
exampleJa: 新しい垢作った
exampleEn: I made a new account.
socialConsequenceEn: Everyday net shorthand. Spawns kagi-aka, ura-aka, sute-aka.

meaningJa: 本音や趣味用の非公開サブ垢
exampleJa: 裏垢でこっそり呟いてる
exampleEn: I quietly post on my secret alt.
socialConsequenceEn: Where the real thoughts go. Everyone suspects everyone has one.

meaningJa: 非公開設定のアカウント
exampleJa: 鍵垢だからフォロー申請して
exampleEn: It's private, so send a follow request.
socialConsequenceEn: The 'lock' icon as a noun. Standard social-media talk.

meaningJa: アカウントが利用停止されること
exampleJa: スパム判定で凍結された
exampleEn: I got suspended for looking like spam.
socialConsequenceEn: Lit. 'frozen'. The dreaded social-platform timeout.

meaningJa: リプライの略
exampleJa: リプ来ないと寂しい
exampleEn: It's lonely when nobody replies.
socialConsequenceEn: Core social-media verb/noun. Spawns 'ripu-ran' (reply chaos).

meaningJa: 情報を広めること、RT
exampleJa: 拡散希望って書いてある
exampleEn: It says 'please share widely'.
socialConsequenceEn: The 'please RT' culture word. Often follows 'kibou' (request).

meaningEn: To charge in / confront (online or off)
meaningJa: 突撃する、直接乗り込むこと
exampleJa: 運営に凸してきた
exampleEn: I went and confronted the admins.
socialConsequenceEn: The kanji 凸 = 'charge'. Can mean a fun call-in or a raid.

meaningJa: 掲示板のスレッド
exampleJa: そのスレもう落ちたよ
exampleEn: That thread's already dead.
socialConsequenceEn: 2ch/5ch backbone vocab. 'Sure ga ochiru' = thread dies.

meaningJa: 匿名の書き込み者
exampleJa: 名無しの意見だけど一理ある
exampleRomaji: nanashi no iken da kedo ichiri aru
exampleEn: Anon's take, but it has a point.
socialConsequenceEn: The anonymous default name on Japanese boards. Pure 2ch.

meaningJa: わざと釣る挑発的な書き込み
exampleJa: 完全に釣りタイトルだった
exampleEn: It was total clickbait.
socialConsequenceEn: Lit. 'fishing'. To get 'tsurareta' = to take the bait.

meaningJa: 冗談に本気で返答すること
exampleJa: ネタにマジレスすんな
exampleEn: Don't seriously reply to a joke.
socialConsequenceEn: Killing the joke by answering it straight. Mildly cringe.

meaningJa: 無視する、受け流す
exampleJa: 煽りはスルーが一番
exampleEn: Best to just ignore the trolls.
socialConsequenceEn: From 'through'. The wise net move: don't feed it.

meaningEn: To read a message and not reply
meaningJa: 既読をつけて返信しないこと
exampleJa: 三日も既読スルーされた
exampleEn: Left on read for three whole days.
socialConsequenceEn: The LINE-era heartbreak. Read receipts made it a thing.

meaningJa: 情報弱者の略
exampleJa: それ情弱が引っかかる詐欺だよ
exampleRomaji: sore joujaku ga hikkakaru sagi da yo
exampleEn: That scam only catches the clueless.
socialConsequenceEn: From 'jouhou jakusha'. A slightly elitist net jab.

meaningJa: 定型文、ひな形
exampleJa: 質問はテンプレ読んでから
exampleEn: Read the template before asking.
socialConsequenceEn: Forum FAQ culture. 'Tenpure-doori' = exactly as expected.

meaningJa: 自作自演。複数人を装うこと
exampleJa: それ自演バレてるよ
exampleEn: Everyone can tell that's you replying to yourself.
socialConsequenceEn: From 'jisaku-jien'. A classic forum accusation.

meaningJa: 他人になりすます行為
exampleJa: なりすましアカウントに注意
exampleEn: Watch out for impersonator accounts.
socialConsequenceEn: Standard security/net term. Fully serious usage.

meaningJa: フォローを外すこと
exampleJa: うざいからリムった
exampleEn: They were annoying so I unfollowed.
socialConsequenceEn: From 'remove' + -ru. The quiet social-media breakup.

meaningJa: フォローを返すこと
exampleJa: フォロバありがとう
exampleEn: Thanks for the follow-back.
socialConsequenceEn: Follow-economy basics. 'Foroba 100%' = follows everyone back.

meaningJa: 相互フォローの関係
exampleJa: 相互さんと絡みたい
exampleEn: I want to chat with my mutuals.
socialConsequenceEn: 'Sougo-san' = your mutuals. Core follow-culture word.

meaningEn: Dead / deserted (a quiet thread or server)
meaningJa: 人がいなくて閑散としている状態
exampleJa: このサーバー過疎ってる
exampleEn: This server is totally dead.
socialConsequenceEn: Borrowed from rural 'depopulation'. Used for dead online spaces.

meaningJa: 情報をまとめたサイトや投稿
exampleJa: まとめサイトで知った
exampleEn: I found out via a summary site.
socialConsequenceEn: 'Matome-site' culture — sometimes informative, sometimes spammy.

meaningJa: 固定ハンドルネーム
exampleJa: あのスレの有名コテハン
exampleEn: A famous named regular on that thread.
socialConsequenceEn: On anonymous boards, using a name stands out. Very 2ch.

meaningJa: 可能性がある、ひょっとしたら
exampleJa: ワンチャン間に合うかも
exampleEn: There's a chance we make it.
socialConsequenceEn: From 'one chance'. Totally current, crosses into spoken slang.

meaningJa: 理解した、了解
exampleJa: 了解、把握した
exampleEn: Roger, got it.
socialConsequenceEn: Net-flavored 'understood'. Crisp, slightly cool.

meaningJa: アップロードの2ch表記
exampleJa: 画像うpよろ
exampleEn: Upload the pic, please.
socialConsequenceEn: Classic 2ch typo-spelling of 'up'. Charmingly retro.

meaningJa: 話の種、冗談、情報源
exampleJa: それただのネタだから
exampleEn: That's just a joke, relax.
socialConsequenceEn: Super versatile: joke, content, or scoop. 'Neta-bare' = spoiler.

meaningJa: アカウントを削除すること
exampleJa: 炎上して垢消しした
exampleEn: Got flamed and deleted my account.
socialConsequenceEn: The nuclear exit. Often follows an 'enjou'.

meaningJa: 笑った、の古いネット表現
exampleJa: 誤字ひどくてワロス
exampleEn: The typo's so bad, lol.
socialConsequenceEn: Mid-2000s board lol. Reads vintage now.
